clean_body: [] Hi Friend, Welcome to the latest New PA Project Education Fund (NPPEF) newsletter! In our previous edition we warned about the slippery slope we were in when it came to deportations. Since, the Trump regime has wrongly detained dozens of U.S. citizens — including a ten-year-old Texas girl [] recovering from brain cancer, a 19-year-old U.S. citizen with learning disabilities who was [] wrongfully incarcerated [] for 10 days in a privately run immigration detention facility in Arizona or a 20-year-old born in Georgia [] , and arrested in Florida. All mistakes, that cause fear in the American population. The deportation of Kilmar Armando Abrego Garcia, who, due to an “administrative error,” was sent to a dangerous and violent mega-prison in El Salvador, does not help ease the fear. The Fifth and Fourteenth Amendments to the Constitution [] establish the right to due process — the Fifth Amendment says that “[n]o person shall ... be deprived of life, liberty, or property, without due process of law.” The Fourteenth Amendment extends this obligation to the states. No PERSON. This is true even for the undocumented, even if someone is here without authorization. Due process for Kilmar Armando Abrego Garcia, should have included notice of deportation and the opportunity to contest it before it happened. Now even as the Supreme Court has ruled for his return, he has yet to be returned home We remember Fannie Lou Hamer's statement: "No one is free until everyone is free," which is more relevant today than ever. REGISTER TO VOTE [] Social Media of the Week [] "I'll tell you what freedom is to me: no fear." – Nina Simone Nina Simone was a classically trained pianist (not common for a young Black woman in the 1950s) who fused her foundation with jazz, gospel, blues, and folk to create a sound entirely her own, defying all genres. She didn’t fit into a box or label, nor did she try to. Simone was denied entry to a prestigious music school, the Curtis Institute of Music in Philadelphia, because of her race. This moment fueled her lifelong work in civil rights. Her songs like “Mississippi Goddam” became an anthem for civil rights and Black empowerment nationally. "An artist's duty is to reflect the times." – Nina Simone #NinaSimone [] #BlackHistory [] #CivilRights [] SHARE ON FACEBOOK [] [] Let's save our planet by saving our democracy. Happy #EarthDay [] from NPPEF - remember you have the choice to voice your vote for leaders and legislations that protect our only home, planet Earth. SHARE ON INSTAGRAM [] [] #CourtsMatter The U.S. Justice Department withdrew its lawsuit [] that accused the heavily Hispanic city of Hazleton in Pennsylvania of illegally diluting the political power of its growing Hispanic population. The department’s initial lawsuit, filed in January, said the “at-large” system of electing city council members, as opposed to electing them by district, was unfair to Hispanic voters and prevented them from getting elected to city council. Hazleton’s 30,000 residents are about two-thirds Hispanic, according to U.S. Census figures. One-third is non-Hispanic white. Closer to Philadelphia, the largest health system in Delaware County began closing two hospitals [] Wednesday after a federal judge in Texas approved their bankruptcy proceedings. The emergency rooms of Taylor Hospital in Ridley Park and Crozer-Chester Medical Center in Upland are expected to close today, Friday, April 25th. Emergency medical services will end next Friday, on May 2. Crozer's ambulatory centers at Brinton Lakes, Broomall, Haverford, and Media are expected to remain open. This November, voters will have the power to retain or vacate three seats of the Pennsylvania Supreme Court. The votes cast by powerful Pennsylvania voters in November will impact our lives for years to come, as Pennsylvania’s Supreme Court will make decisions about our election administration, immigrant rights. access to abortion, the safety of our workplaces, how bankruptcies are handled, and the right to unionize. Make sure you can voice your vote! CHECK YOUR REGISTRATION [] [] We are hiring! Let's provide civic education together! We're looking for people passionate about voting rights in Erie, Lawrence, Lehigh Valley, Bucks, Montgomery, Chester, and Delaware County to help us shape the future of Pennsylvania. Join our team and help us provide civic education in our communities and register folks to vote! Positions start at $20 an hour. Interested? Contact jobs@nppef.org EMAIL US [jobs@nppef.org] News You Can Use State Rep. David Rowe (R-Union) wants to introduce a bill to make it easier for hunters and fishermen to get registered to vote [] . He represents a lot of sportsmen in the rural 85th state House District, which includes parts of Union, Juniata, and Mifflin counties and all of Snyder County. “The bill will not complicate the process of getting a license. The bill will not add another step to the hunting license process,” Rowe told Capital-Star [] . “Rather, it will simply make it more accessible. So, for sportsmen that are going to a state office, there will be paper registration forms available for them to simply grab.” As a voting rights organization, we applaud all legislation that would make it easier for people to register to vote, and make our voices to be heard at the ballot box. Meanwhile, Pennsylvania’s first two openly autistic legislators, State Reps. Jessica Behnam (D-Allegheny) and Abigail Sailsbury (D-Allegheny), condemned the comments made by the Department of Health Secretary, Robert F. Kennedy Jr., attacking autistic people [] . They are also opposed to his plan to collect private medical records from US citizens to research autism. Remember that slippery slope regarding immigrant deportations? And if you are waiting for a tax return but have defaulted student loans, there is a chance the government will garnish your return and even your wages. The Department of Education announced Monday it will restart collections on defaulted federal student loans on May 5 [] , including potentially garnishing wages for millions of workers. The Treasury Offset Program allows the government to withhold federal payments such as tax returns for individuals in default. After a 30-day notice, the federal government could also begin garnishing a borrower’s wages. So make sure to check your statement. NPPEF on the Ground [] Engaging the young Regional Organizing Director, Daniel Levin providing civil education to students. Did you know that as long as a student is 18 years of age on or before the day of the next election - they can register to vote? Many 17 year old students don't know this and might pass up the opportunity to register to vote, thinking they have to be 18 at time of registering. It's our passion to bridge the gap here on civic education. #YourVoiceYourPowerYourVote [] #GoVotePA [] #StudentVoters [] GET REGISTERED ! [] Chester County [] [] [] Several students register to vote at a high school in Coatesville. York County [] [] Canvassers on the streets of York Dauphin County [] [] [] Meeting people where they are, be it on the bus, the park or the gym Super voters vote twice a year, every year ! Thank you for doing your part to stay informed and engaged! [] Last day to register to vote: Monday, May 5, 2025 Primary Election: Tuesday, May 20, 2025 Last day to register to vote for the General Election: Monday, October 20, 2025 Last day to request mail-in ballot: Tuesday, October 28, 2025 General Election: Tuesday, November 4, 2025 Important Links: Voter Hotlines: PA Department of State Website [vote.pa.gov] Voter Protection Hotline: 1-866-OUR-VOTE Upcoming elections [] DOS PA Voter Hotline: 1-877-VOTESPA Mail-in and Absentee Ballot [] Spanish/English Hotline: 1-866-VE-Y-VOTA Fact-Checking Election Claims [] Asian Languages Hotline: 1-888-API-VOTE Arabic/English Hotline: 1-844-YALLA-US Paid for by the New PA Project Education Fund New PA Project Education Fund PO Box 453 West Chester, PA 19381 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: .

name: Juanita, United We Dream
subject: [🔔 Merch Drop Alert] WE power our communities!
body:
Order your exclusive “Powered by the People” merch today!🧢✨ | | | Hey Peter, It’s time to wear it with pride: Our communities are Powered by The People![**Check out our exclusive new merch line that celebrates the stories of working people who fuel our communities every day.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMVUu0AIBZu1MoDI01Ek7UpsywLqZXWxx7fRaVnjqUz4dkZ2MAAk8rh24ZfIVVI5rLVYVAkD-buDdlbX9kxWohPpFNAG62H2VVdlrWkG9BglnjdjnJnt6zp5sd0wcbQV4nnDpH-Dn3dzv2qQFjDR5kbkNBl4vgcHjFtztWgspcJ4Yqr-2i-gLuKWT80wmybOgIHmlOTnj2qYRykr8QEjudcg_iz8D8ufyfSleMRh53x4PWEpzNNpE_B8KqzWilrp1s-f4K4s8vtqSWV2Eusw6ni4/4gb/WSyfkfHVTW-HRkyhqwUj6A/h0/h001.k51gt1JDY5AroEOf5P-bejzJYN9HXfKIFPt5hBkgqjg) From the friend you call when you’re stranded on the side of the road to the teacher who inspired you to believe in yourself growing up: **Our communities have**** _always_****been powered by immigrants.**
* 💪Powered by the courage and dedication of immigrant workers who show up every day with the goal of taking care of our families and communities
* 🧑🍼Powered by our parents who dreamed of watching us grow up feeling safe and loved
* 🏳️⚧️Powered by our undocuqueer and trans friends who know their lives are sacred and refuse to be driven into the shadows
* ❤️🔥Powered by the resilience of young people who dare to be bold in our demands for a future that leaves no one behind

Let your wardrobe speak of what we’ve always known about our communities: _We_ protect one another, _we_ build our future, and _we_ belong here! clean_body: Order your exclusive “Powered by the People” merch today!🧢✨ | | | Hey Peter, It’s time to wear it with pride: Our communities are Powered by The People From the friend you call when you’re stranded on the side of the road to the teacher who inspired you to believe in yourself growing up: Our communities have alwaysbeen powered by immigrants. 💪Powered by the courage and dedication of immigrant workers who show up every day with the goal of taking care of our families and communities 🧑🍼Powered by our parents who dreamed of watching us grow up feeling safe and loved 🏳️⚧️Powered by our undocuqueer and trans friends who know their lives are sacred and refuse to be driven into the shadows ❤️🔥Powered by the resilience of young people who dare to be bold in our demands for a future that leaves no one behind Let your wardrobe speak of what we’ve always known about our communities: We protect one another, we build our future, and we belong here! Order your “Powered by The People” shirt and hat today: We want to see you rep your shirt and hat! Be sure to tag @UnitedWeDream to show us how you style your merch. We know the strength, resilience, and hard work that immigrant people contribute to our communities across the country. But right now, our classmates and neighbors are being abducted, torn away from our schools and families, and being put into detention hundreds of miles away — leaving an unmistakable absence in our hearts. Only by coming together — to heal, to cry, to re-energize – can we face this moment and fight for the future we need. 🤝 to remind the world that as long as our communities continue showing up for one another, we’re READY to fight for a future that protects us all. All proceeds will fuel our work to share the stories of immigrant people who power our communities. In power, Juanita and the UWD team | Follow us on: | | | | |

| Peter,

We recently marked the end of Trump’s first 100 days in office — let’s look at how he did: [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107346094/544390377/-1859967770?source=EM_NBI_DWSPAC_20250508_EN_Fascism_0_3MO_DWSPAC_NA_NA_Fascism1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzkvMS84ODcwOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiZDdjN2Q5Zi01MjJjLWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Xzn-dbJdTUvEPiuS0VCJlcsT6J_binqvTmCW91T37Kc=&emci=af097398-522c-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&emdi=bd7c7d9f-522c-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=5260669) The grades are in — _**Trump is failing badly.**_

President Trump has made it clear from _**DAY ONE**_ that he does not value the American people or our democratic values. He continues to ignore real problems, hurt working families, and put billionaires at the front of his agenda.

Let’s take a look at the promises **_he failed to keep:_**
* Lowering the cost of healthcare
* Growing our economy
* Taking necessary climate action
* Respecting education
* Growing the workforce
* Raising the minimum wage

Peter, Trump is failing America. **[What grade would you give Trump for his first 100 days in office?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107346095/544390378/-1859967770?source=EM_NBI_DWSPAC_20250508_EN_Fascism_0_3MO_DWSPAC_NA_NA_Fascism1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzkvMS84ODcwOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiZDdjN2Q5Zi01MjJjLWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Xzn-dbJdTUvEPiuS0VCJlcsT6J_binqvTmCW91T37Kc=&emci=af097398-522c-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&emdi=bd7c7d9f-522c-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=5260669)** | | ****_******[GRADE TRUMP](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107346096/544390379/-1859967770?source=EM_NBI_DWSPAC_20250508_EN_Fascism_0_3MO_DWSPAC_NA_NA_Fascism1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzkvMS84ODcwOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiZDdjN2Q5Zi01MjJjLWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Xzn-dbJdTUvEPiuS0VCJlcsT6J_binqvTmCW91T37Kc=&emci=af097398-522c-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&emdi=bd7c7d9f-522c-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=5260669)******_****

clean_body: Please don’t ignore this message >> | = Hi Peter, it’s Ritchie – I’ll make this short and sweet. Here are the facts: FACT: There are only three seats hanging between Republican and Democratic control of the House. FACT: I don’t do a second of call time, so this campaign is by and for the people. FACT: We need to raise $25,000 this month to meet our May goal and protect our common-sense, progressive agenda in Congress. Taking back the House, saving Social Security and Medicare (and more!), and limiting Trump’s damage to our Democracy are absolutely critical objectives right now. That’s why I desperately need supporters like you to step up, Peter: = | = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = = = Thank you for helping us turn things around for this people-powered movement, Ritchie | | Paid for by Torres For Congress PO Box 580303, Bronx, NY 10458 www.torres.nyc Ritchie Torres represents the future of the Democratic Party. Raised by a single mother in public housing, Ritchie personally understands the hardships that working families endure. At age 25, Ritchie made history as the youngest elected official to serve on the New York City Council. He fought tirelessly for the working families of The Bronx, and now, he's fighting for those families and for progressive values in Congress. He is the first openly gay Afro Latino ever elected to Congress. Ritchie is a champion for progressive policies to fight poverty and to improve the lives of families just like the one he was raised in. Peter, please, consider making a =. I didn’t run for Congress to be part of the political circus; I came to deliver results for West Michigan – to work across the aisle, protect our freedoms, and bring real accountability back to Washington.

| **Team, I’ve only been in Congress for 2 years – and even in that short time, I’ve seen the impacts Republicans' chaos and dysfunction have had on working families and our democratic process.**

From supporting billions of dollars in cuts to Social Security and Medicare to shutting down critical international aid and standing by as the Trump administration attacks our constitution and right to due process, it’s clear Republicans are only focused on fueling division and scoring political points.

That’s not what I signed up for. And I know it’s not what you voted for.

I didn’t run for Congress to be part of the political circus; I came to deliver results for West Michigan – to work across the aisle, protect our freedoms, and bring real accountability back to Washington.

**National experts are calling Michigan’s 3rd District one of the most competitive in the nation. With Republicans clinging to a historically slim House majority, this seat will be a firewall as we work to fight back against Republican chaos and dysfunction.**

name: Byron Donalds
subject: My mother would’ve ripped DEI to shreds
body:
She always taught me our country was built on hard work
| | | | |
---
[](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=6002de980e7c49808e75f19766cce279&_e=si88c6JsZznLkXSYy-2oK9W91bn_63WbNmrl-1CXUHo7VZUbd0qSQNw0bfgQ7_SyQL375CzUYimatF572x9C9yy5fAMEklKvarcXws-ure9_N_U-fLuRo8E3mDoaDdu8d9hN_1f6JCRlq-lq6eNqFNVvHXtv3Il_IEiqTT2k1vu_82lga4bx3RLZmLkLiWvwj7gxPUm7UUyt9s-IXRRgMzTgPfiwFBNiaABvopEF8Ao662hrYKztp4yp_-pErw1Z-qAtd12ZKxBjLWK8XrH62dlqGSbHW3vs8APKxG91b2cbbJhQVe1PerVwxy36rh4A3wWwFhKt1QZJh6SyHdNxbKI6mzAVHsobAW1ClMRtx6ITKfF9UrUfwnRWuDgZzaCAIeNXHxxoJVbuq_6HmnTRALoFCM9j49JIy9voNXg5heluZsCVTRlXNyAIDpOdouiL)
|
---
I was raised by a single mother.
I learned to fight with my back against the wall with no way out but through.
So when the radical left Democrats get up in my face & call me a “race-traitor” after I applauded Trump for abolishing DEI, I’m left speechless.
My mom _**never**_ taught me to hate my fellow Americans.
She always instilled in me the values of _hard work_ and _merit._
**If I told her I was a _victim_ like someone in the Woke Mob, she’d probably just laugh like it’s some kind of joke.**
**That’s the America I believe in**. And that’s why I’m a MAGA Patriot who loves President Trump and the America First Movement.
But after I stood with President Trump as he abolished DEI, the attacks against me have been horrendous.
The race-obsessed left just sees me as another Black Man who should sit down, shut up, and vote blue for the rest of my life.
Their hypocrisy _disgusts_ me, and I can brush off most of their attacks, but here’s the _**[real problem](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=6002de980e7c49808e75f19766cce279&_e=si88c6JsZznLkXSYy-2oK9W91bn_63WbNmrl-1CXUHo7VZUbd0qSQNw0bfgQ7_SyQL375CzUYimatF572x9C9yy5fAMEklKvarcXws-ure9_N_U-fLuRo8E3mDoaDdu8d9hN_1f6JCRlq-lq6eNqFNVvHXtv3Il_IEiqTT2k1vu_82lga4bx3RLZmLkLiWvwj7gxPUm7UUyt9s-IXRRgMzTgPfiwFBNiaABvopEF8Ao662hrYKztp4yp_-pErw1Z-qAtd12ZKxBjLWK8XrH62dlqGSbHW3vs8APKxG91b2cyUD1ogTgicJ7OhCrX3cly2PFq2rc9GhL9vBDj5Y_jEnuIR0BpNjEAi1vHH3vNMpSTzSPgag9N6s-z9oZq2wPRtU2FK-tTfvOrY7R5-dvYv6SUaxYt1kj5g7SkKaFc9p7ktX4jHf7oIgnpKYZf_om9)**_ that I can’t solve on my own.
Right now I’m running for Governor in Florida, and truth be told the radical-left is dropping the hammer on me. They’re prepared to spend **millions of dollars** to prop-up a Democrat that could flip MAGA’s hometurf **BLUE**.
I’m committed to being President Trump’s STRONGEST Governor ally in the nation. But I can’t do this alone.
_**[So before the day is over, can I count on your support to keep me in the fight?](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=6002de980e7c49808e75f19766cce279&_e=si88c6JsZznLkXSYy-2oK9W91bn_63WbNmrl-1CXUHo7VZUbd0qSQNw0bfgQ7_SyQL375CzUYimatF572x9C9yy5fAMEklKvarcXws-ure9_N_U-fLuRo8E3mDoaDdu8d9hN_1f6JCRlq-lq6eNqFNVvHXtv3Il_IEiqTT2k1vu_82lga4bx3RLZmLkLiWvwj7gxPUm7UUyt9s-IXRRgMzTgPfiwFBNiaABvopEF8Ao662hrYKztp4yp_-pErw1Z-qAtd12ZKxBjLWK8XrH62dlqGSbHW3vs8APKxG91b2d5PPTosZUIBUbh6vFs3CghUy7Lw7IYGAQHkdx2ExqMFaBlsJ1ap_UfL6-oyJCba5AFJDtiLyD6psZnER5YM8tML8Uf7Q5cGkFzuOSbUQYP1TGmI6GQs6b0BF1xPwWXGLnw5_OHQLNFk3zPGo1sDS4C)**_

|

But I can’t do this alone. | | God bless you, Byron Donalds | | | | | | | | | | | | Paid by Byron Donalds, Republican, for Governor 10941 Harmony Park Drive, Suite 4 Bonita Springs, FL 34135 %

name: GOTV PAC
subject: Trump has brought fascism to America. Here’s how we stop it:
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY GET OUT THE VOTE PAC NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR ANY CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email3182025&amount=25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email3182025&amount=25]
Peter ,
May 8, 1945. Victory in Europe Day. It was a moment when, all across the Allied world, people took to the streets — not to celebrate conquest but to mark the end of a brutal, authoritarian nightmare that had nearly swallowed our planet.
But it wasn't only a military victory. It was a political one, too. A collective decision that fascism had no place in a free society.
Today, on VE Day, we remember that moment not just to honor the past but because the threat we once stood together to defeat is showing itself again, this time, here at home.
The MAGA movement isn't some fringe curiosity or populist sideshow. It's a coordinated assault on democracy powered by grievance, nationalism, and a willingness to abandon legal and ethical limits in the pursuit of power.
Remember: Donald Trump has dined with open neo-Nazis, and praised Adolf Hitler as “smart”, welcomed fascist salutes at his own inauguration, and he has insulted the memory of American veterans who gave their lives at Normandy, calling them “suckers” and “losers.” Meanwhile, Trump loyalists have already tried to overturn an election, and right now, they're banning books, rewriting history, and targeting vulnerable communities with glee. If given the chance, we know they'll go further.
That's why GOTV is fighting tough elections today in San Antonio, Virginia, Pennsylvania, and New Jersey, and anywhere else we can find to defeat MAGA extremism at the ballot box.
But we cannot do it without your help.
Will you donate $25 today to help GOTV PAC stop the rise of American authoritarianism in its tracks? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email05082025&amount=25]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
DONATE $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=Email05082025] DONATE $35 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?express_lane=true&amount=35&refcode=Email05082025]
DONATE $60 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?express_lane=true&amount=60&refcode=Email05082025] DONATE $120 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?express_lane=true&amount=120&refcode=Email05082025]
OTHER AMOUNT → [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email05082025]
VE Day wasn't the end of the story. It was a reminder that democracy is fragile — and that the only thing that defeats fascism is people standing up, together, and saying: not here, not now, not ever again.
GOTV PAC
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email05082025&amount=25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email05082025&amount=25]
Get Out the Vote PAC mobilizes voters often overlooked by mainstream campaigns to help Democratic candidates win in the most competitive districts.
Our professional phone bankers specialize in long- and short-term GOTV campaigns, event registration, voting assistance in heavily gerrymandered states, fundraising, and call time management. Our mission is to flip red districts blue, any elected position from dog walker to Congress member.
Learn more at: gotv.vote [https://www.gotv.vote/]

GOTV PAC
712 H St NE

Washington, DC 20002
United States

Our mission is to Flip the House by supporting targeted Congressional districts and down-ballot races within those districts. Our internal research evidences that the impact of a Dollar invested in this manner amplifies the top and bottom of the ticket.

* **TOP NEWS**

"**Texas braces for surge in power demand amid population and industry boom** ," Texas Tribune's **Alejandra Martinez** \-- "Texas is preparing for an unprecedented surge in electricity demand, with projections showing the need for nearly double the state grid's power capacity within the next six years.

The state's main electric grid operator, the Electric Reliability Council of Texas or ERCOT, attributes the forecast in part to a wave of large-scale industrial development from data centers, hydrogen production and oil and gas operations transitioning to electrification. Population growth and extreme weather events also contribute to the state's increasing energy needs.

Despite a 30% increase in generation capacity over the past four years -- largely driven by the growth of solar, wind, and battery storage -- energy experts who gathered at a Texas Tribune event at Dallas College at the Cedar Valley campus Thursday said the grid must adapt fast to meet growing demand.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/52bc911f-8951-442e-8a25-4687cd93da73?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
They said the state's big challenges include legislative uncertainties and keeping residents' electric bills from skyrocketing.

"If anyone could do it, it'll be Texas. Like we're faster than every other state. We're faster than most regions in the world," **Michael E. Webber** , an energy policy expert at the University of Texas at Austin, said. "If we don't get in our own way."
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/52bc911f-8951-442e-8a25-4687cd93da73?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The event, moderated by Texas Tribune politics reporter **Kayla Guo** , explored how Texas can meet this moment: preparing its grid and infrastructure for a dramatic increase in demand, managing costs and ensuring reliability for Texans. Guo was also joined by**Sandra Haverlah** , president of the Texas Consumer Association and **Garrett Golding** , assistant vice president for energy programs at the Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas.

For consumers, the stakes are high. Haverlah said that residential electrical bills will reflect the cost of building new grid infrastructure and upgrading and maintaining existing facilities and equipment.

"For many years, that grid wasn't upgraded, or those particular lines and poles weren't upgraded, but now we're looking at storms that are worse and people are upset about outages," Haverlah said. "People want the equipment upgraded, but the equipment is now more expensive."

The panel also discussed [Senate Bill 6](https://substack.com/redirect/529ab07e-8307-40a6-9557-c46a17382e24?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by Sen.**Phil King** , R-Weatherford, a high priority bill that would place new obligations on large electricity users, give state regulators more tools to oversee how and when those businesses connect to the grid and help ERCOT better forecast the electricity needs to avoid situations like the catastrophic 2021 winter storm.

ERCOT lost control of the state's power supply during Winter Storm Uri in February 2021, leaving millions without electricity and causing widespread blackouts that lasted days. Hundreds of Texans died. The storm left many concerned about the grid's reliability and those concerns still linger years later.

The bill would require large electricity users like data companies to disclose competitive information about where else they might be considering locating. Webber said that is causing nervousness among tech companies like Microsoft, because it could impact their business strategy.

Data center companies are also worried about giving the state control over their operations and disclosing their energy needs.

Webber said that while data center companies are generally willing to reduce power usage during peak hours, several are uncomfortable with the prospect of political interference in their business operations." | | Dear Fellow Conservative,

Next year will mark the 250th anniversary of The Declaration of Independence.

This revolutionary document laid down the foundation of American political thought. It's one of the most important documents in human history and its anniversary is certainly worth celebrating.
---
|
[](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/c/JEOL44BNT62XcMa0nWNjj36GfsZUUawTv6IfzVAS977uhzSYtxE3cM_5BIb5Ac-67IQfnqWH9N2zfM5R88JQsoqBIp6SYFoyQLIQKZy5cBTZlzVyk0m3ryqKsU9CWHvqZZlKTwnDk2xWaFEWqcYEV-czPiMhitbm1EBGg8w06EIcTmXxVG9nOhl4C84qEKBZOwHabSynbkLNTRxmYiQdt2oRaQR1TmsLLN-UpcA6An4Z32Y8ijAqtImieoN7WbV2X-pV0jm2-ikKSr-NuCpixh4AjtXKpGeWXYmWwm0UzMOMCPl-PuA-A-LNjJ0dlLjN69exzsIODXh3ZoiZb0AhQ5LHqWyE015cxBBYBinzdPUO6lzokBsmfDzZA-7L1smYwpCzVQZED6269A9k3lnAfsVm402dUkYhlseD1JJIjT8li0DR5qrwm_cxReERfL_nmw/CeVIUNdoh8CNeemXyh4uJJNAhJsk3k4e/8)
---
| | To help celebrate, I'd like to offer you a free copy of The Heritage Foundation's new eBook, the [_**Guide to the Declaration of Independence**_](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/c/Ee_TNlDr_Lw5QzJ8iULAHG7dpM4xRse54er8ZPJJDzATu7YQmTx9FQKiip56vFA_n_t5jPccHnWJaIfsw-XD6_p_9oDeNM0Ze327L9EcganzoKSoQ9a9Lm_vQNZzIFFbxaZY5XRK-1fvMoioHjTJ80hy-9IIpO8YzZUys_RVyRU56FiWmuLmlq6ROqO1PyTyVb3H2R6GWPPBAlmNb-BBqoL-M_k1G99KDYAukn7jw4Qj2NuJRlPsykFlh-JNSLBP2-_vI7AEK0dU064dMdOGUyd4CSwrrCrV3rk-YeEP5pUKJTHq5o2flKZwqFJAd4TJ8KmdKneOh74Vyyjhf_zKwHSJJzqvNtiVwvMFk1kKz_bVvjLd7QfGWkV4kQH5ZQ37Pdo1rNsyTduXoWW2oL10208B54LBlbk2Hn8orpfdK6zYfg7-CjBZQN-SeX7qlA_Clw/9kQkgZt-JGvDY-4jYbYFH4Vgz_fXMkfu/8).

This FREE eBook breaks down this important founding document's history, including:
* Why our Founders decided to declare independence.
* The writing process for the Declaration.
* The philosophical tradition that informed our Founding Fathers' thinking.
* How the Declaration affected American politics.
* And much more!

This eBook is an essential tool for all American patriots who want to learn more about our nation's founding principles.

**You can get your FREE digital copy using the secure link below.**

[**https://secured.heritage.org/guide-to-the-declaration-ebook/**](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/c/z0CHiSMUSy95xMlwKM9Er2n5q5m_ej-b8cT9Uq-lcszFn23nFl6G1riXrUa-L0rLXG_1c1gODAkxBr63Xe3PSVoLJJbjLR5JgtRZ--_JWtYiKMSyzCIbm4GoIkGm5HPkYGwI_rsEAzHY7n1RWtt38pnSlBbQsjZudzZSftZQRol8G-pJ0YDLE0h3S-oYC6v2ErD_UyRom6JF2ca7LptVKXAsB-mA4Q_1Q4VbFygrmOQZEv7D7vYw8jgL3o6Pw_BG3y79EgTiUXwUm6vSNPqpHV3zRhG159o6f_hIzMJ0iazthjeS0zX-9fo69XSNSmhCRuax_BC4Xc4knE4YlXeLOVONFQYSXhijGoQsbKCSF_jZ1Nj1_UVNX-3SjUwgHbfljdLc90nBeGseB5kFj5zk82gFY2cW00w7WZjPUph95Re9yxFUzUm-TVy5d3AMILFXig/dW-F8uw_xmhJlhtbeAtbB1o6UcHyZ9EW/8)

I hope this eBook helps you celebrate America's 250th.

Sincerely,

Kevin D. Roberts, Ph.D.
President, The Heritage Foundation

> Read the most recent FREE subscriber email [here](https://substack.com/redirect/00c18354-be2a-4c12-b871-286e5024bb4d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
> Become a PAID subscriber for $5/mo or $50/yr [here](https://substack.com/redirect/cad6381f-faba-41ef-8b35-4d84a4af8f29?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* * *
* **TOP NEWS**

"**Texas braces for surge in power demand amid population and industry boom** ," Texas Tribune's **Alejandra Martinez** \-- "Texas is preparing for an unprecedented surge in electricity demand, with projections showing the need for nearly double the state grid's power capacity within the next six years.

The state's main electric grid operator, the Electric Reliability Council of Texas or ERCOT, attributes the forecast in part to a wave of large-scale industrial development from data centers, hydrogen production and oil and gas operations transitioning to electrification. Population growth and extreme weather events also contribute to the state's increasing energy needs.

Despite a 30% increase in generation capacity over the past four years -- largely driven by the growth of solar, wind, and battery storage -- energy experts who gathered at a Texas Tribune event at Dallas College at the Cedar Valley campus Thursday said the grid must adapt fast to meet growing demand.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/5a162910-29ab-414c-9171-dacee3007173?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
They said the state's big challenges include legislative uncertainties and keeping residents' electric bills from skyrocketing.

"If anyone could do it, it'll be Texas. Like we're faster than every other state. We're faster than most regions in the world," **Michael E. Webber** , an energy policy expert at the University of Texas at Austin, said. "If we don't get in our own way."
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/5a162910-29ab-414c-9171-dacee3007173?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The event, moderated by Texas Tribune politics reporter **Kayla Guo** , explored how Texas can meet this moment: preparing its grid and infrastructure for a dramatic increase in demand, managing costs and ensuring reliability for Texans. Guo was also joined by**Sandra Haverlah** , president of the Texas Consumer Association and **Garrett Golding** , assistant vice president for energy programs at the Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas.

For consumers, the stakes are high. Haverlah said that residential electrical bills will reflect the cost of building new grid infrastructure and upgrading and maintaining existing facilities and equipment.

"For many years, that grid wasn't upgraded, or those particular lines and poles weren't upgraded, but now we're looking at storms that are worse and people are upset about outages," Haverlah said. "People want the equipment upgraded, but the equipment is now more expensive."

The panel also discussed [Senate Bill 6](https://substack.com/redirect/08cfc01c-05ee-469b-9ff0-9f53e8f0ba51?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by Sen.**Phil King** , R-Weatherford, a high priority bill that would place new obligations on large electricity users, give state regulators more tools to oversee how and when those businesses connect to the grid and help ERCOT better forecast the electricity needs to avoid situations like the catastrophic 2021 winter storm.

ERCOT lost control of the state's power supply during Winter Storm Uri in February 2021, leaving millions without electricity and causing widespread blackouts that lasted days. Hundreds of Texans died. The storm left many concerned about the grid's reliability and those concerns still linger years later.

The bill would require large electricity users like data companies to disclose competitive information about where else they might be considering locating. Webber said that is causing nervousness among tech companies like Microsoft, because it could impact their business strategy.

Data center companies are also worried about giving the state control over their operations and disclosing their energy needs.

Webber said that while data center companies are generally willing to reduce power usage during peak hours, several are uncomfortable with the prospect of political interference in their business operations." TOP NEWS "Texas braces for surge in power demand amid population and industry boom ," Texas Tribune's Alejandra Martinez \-- "Texas is preparing for an unprecedented surge in electricity demand, with projections showing the need for nearly double the state grid's power capacity within the next six years. The state's main electric grid operator, the Electric Reliability Council of Texas or ERCOT, attributes the forecast in part to a wave of large-scale industrial development from data centers, hydrogen production and oil and gas operations transitioning to electrification. Population growth and extreme weather events also contribute to the state's increasing energy needs. Despite a 30% increase in generation capacity over the past four years -- largely driven by the growth of solar, wind, and battery storage -- energy experts who gathered at a Texas Tribune event at Dallas College at the Cedar Valley campus Thursday said the grid must adapt fast to meet growing demand. They said the state's big challenges include legislative uncertainties and keeping residents' electric bills from skyrocketing. "If anyone could do it, it'll be Texas. Like we're faster than every other state. We're faster than most regions in the world," Michael E. Webber , an energy policy expert at the University of Texas at Austin, said. "If we don't get in our own way." The event, moderated by Texas Tribune politics reporter Kayla Guo , explored how Texas can meet this moment: preparing its grid and infrastructure for a dramatic increase in demand, managing costs and ensuring reliability for Texans. Guo was also joined bySandra Haverlah , president of the Texas Consumer Association and Garrett Golding , assistant vice president for energy programs at the Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas. For consumers, the stakes are high. Haverlah said that residential electrical bills will reflect the cost of building new grid infrastructure and upgrading and maintaining existing facilities and equipment. "For many years, that grid wasn't upgraded, or those particular lines and poles weren't upgraded, but now we're looking at storms that are worse and people are upset about outages," Haverlah said. "People want the equipment upgraded, but the equipment is now more expensive." The panel also discussed by Sen.Phil King , R-Weatherford, a high priority bill that would place new obligations on large electricity users, give state regulators more tools to oversee how and when those businesses connect to the grid and help ERCOT better forecast the electricity needs to avoid situations like the catastrophic 2021 winter storm. ERCOT lost control of the state's power supply during Winter Storm Uri in February 2021, leaving millions without electricity and causing widespread blackouts that lasted days. Hundreds of Texans died. The storm left many concerned about the grid's reliability and those concerns still linger years later. The bill would require large electricity users like data companies to disclose competitive information about where else they might be considering locating. Webber said that is causing nervousness among tech companies like Microsoft, because it could impact their business strategy. Data center companies are also worried about giving the state control over their operations and disclosing their energy needs. Webber said that while data center companies are generally willing to reduce power usage during peak hours, several are uncomfortable with the prospect of political interference in their business operations." name: Jayapal HQ
subject: re: MAGA’s regressive, billionaire-first budget
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
The Republican budget puts working people last.
# [](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/892?refcode=em20250508-5024-footer&t=1&akid=5024%2E420207%2EFdBopK)
Peter, House Republicans are in the process of writing their budget, and it’s not pretty.
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-20250508?refcode=em20250508-5024&amount=3&refcode2=5024_420207_FdBopK&akid=5024%2E420207%2EFdBopK)
**Here’s just _some_ of what’s in the MAGA budget:**
* Huge cuts to student loan programs that will make borrowing even more expensive for students

* Massive new Pentagon spending and $45 billion for immigration detention centers, including “family detention centers”

* Winding down Wall Street oversight

* Large potential cuts to Medicaid, SNAP, and other critical programs

* Enormous tax cuts for giant corporations and the mega-wealthy

**We need to mobilize our movement and make it clear to Republicans that there will be a political price to pay for supporting this regressive, billionaire-first agenda — and we’re counting on your support to do it.**
[**Peter, if you’re with us in the fight for a government that puts working people first, will you pitch in $3 to support Pramila’s work for the progressive movement?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-20250508?refcode=em20250508-5024&amount=3&refcode2=5024_420207_FdBopK&akid=5024%2E420207%2EFdBopK)

subject: Jocelyn is fighting back against voter suppression
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JOCELYN BENSON FOR GOVERNOR
body:
Jocelyn has worked hard to make Michigan’s elections the most secure and accessible in the nation.
# [](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/203?t=1&akid=288%2E181023%2Ect5tew)
Team — As a leading voting rights expert, we can count on Jocelyn to stand up for Michiganders’ access to the ballot box.
**That’s why she’s been so vocal in her opposition to the _SAVE Act_ — disastrous legislation from the GOP House majority and the Trump administration to make voting more difficult for millions of Americans.**
[](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/239?c0=jmb-em-q22025.rGq0NW&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250508_PM1_V1_S1&t=2&akid=288%2E181023%2Ect5tew)
[**As our next governor, she will continue to stand up for Michiganders’ voting rights. Pitch in to help elect her today!**](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/239?c0=jmb-em-q22025.rGq0NW&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250508_PM1_V1_S1&t=3&akid=288%2E181023%2Ect5tew)
**[DONATE](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/239?c0=jmb-em-q22025.rGq0NW&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250508_PM1_V1_S1&t=4&akid=288%2E181023%2Ect5tew)**
Jocelyn has worked hard to make Michigan’s elections the most secure and accessible in the nation. And, in the face of threats to both herself and her colleagues, Jocelyn oversaw two of the _highest turnout_ and _most transparent_ elections in the state’s history.
That’s why she’s fighting against the _SAVE Act_ — it doesn’t do anything helpful to encourage participation in our democracy.
Instead, it makes it harder for millions of Americans — including Black Michiganders, active-duty troops, and the more than 2.2 million women in Michigan whose married names are not the same name on their birth certificates — to vote.
**That’s not the kind of leadership we need.[Chip in today to help Jocelyn fight for voting rights and defend our democracy as Michigan’s next governor. ](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/239?c0=jmb-em-q22025.rGq0NW&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250508_PM1_V1_S1&t=5&akid=288%2E181023%2Ect5tew)**

subject: You + Fighting Oligarchy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
We want to hear from you.
#
Alexandria has been on a tour of red, blue, and purple districts across the country alongside Bernie Sanders since March.
Now, they’re received over 500 requests to visit working class areas that are furious with their representatives, and will announce more tour dates soon.
But before they go out on the road again, **we want to hear from you** :
[What questions — big or small — do you have for Alexandria and Bernie about what’s next for the country as the Fighting Oligarchy Tour continues?](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/survey/2025-fighting-oligarchy-survey/?source=em20250508-8794-P_FND_SUR_FO&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=8794%2E2526796%2ECE6QtD)

name: Kathryn Harvey
subject: A Hopeful Reflection on Civil Discourse
body:
The end of the 2025 South Carolina Legislative Session.

| | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/66db17ad0e4f/kathryn-harvey-for-congresscampaign-kickoff-6419401?e=edbde6c9b1)
---
|
---
|
---
| Welcome to Keeping Up with Kathryn Harvey. A weekly digest covering South Carolina politics and opportunities for engagement and action in the Upstate.
---
|
# A Hopeful Reflection on Civil Discourse
---
| | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=20939ab2e6&e=edbde6c9b1)
| The power of convening folks for meaningful dialogue has been on my mind quite a bit recently. As someone who has spent their entire career in strategic communications, I’ve observed both troubling trends and reasons for hope in how we engage with one another on matters of public importance. Politically, it can feel as though we are more divided than ever.
However, I have seen glimmers of light here in the Upstate. This past week, Wofford College committed itself as an institution that values diversity of thought, launching [“A Candid Conversation”](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=b3abeaa1a9&e=edbde6c9b1) series that I have had the pleasure to help host. Our goal is to bring folks together for a moderated discussion aimed at highlighting diverse perspectives while encouraging civil discourse.Thank you to both Bakari Sellers and Trey Gowdy for participating in our first installation. Please save the date for **September 29th** , where we will be proudly hosting **The Sister Senators**.
---
|
---
| On [**Frogmore Stew**](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=f79efdfe02&e=edbde6c9b1) this week, we are focusing on this light. Grace Cowan sat down with Upstate’s very own Bill Webster for a rich conversation on how politics has evolved from civil compromise to deep polarization. They explore the legacy of South Carolina political figures, the rise of personalized politics, and what it takes to build respectful dialogue across party lines. Stay tuned for later this week, tomorrow’s episode features a personal conversation with Bakari Sellers.
---
[|
---](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=898156b6b3&e=edbde6c9b1)
| [Listen to Billy’s episode on Frogmore Stew](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=919f331db5&e=edbde6c9b1)
---
| | | | | As we come to an arduous end of South Carolina’s legislative session, I am dedicated to holding space for candid conversations amid our polarized political climate. It is more important than ever. Consider joining me locally, on May 20th, for the first [Tiny Town Hall at Middle Tyger Library.](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=988941e1f2&e=edbde6c9b1)
---
|
---
|
---
| | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=2c31f439a1&e=edbde6c9b1)
| | **Fighting for Freedom Fundraiser with Representative Justin Pearson****May 29 at 6:00 pm Chapman Cultural Center** Join us for a special evening featuring Rep Justin J. Pearson of the Tennessee Three in support of the Spartanburg Young Democrats. The reception starts at 6:00 PM, followed by a special program honoring our young change makers in the community and a keynote by Rep Justin J. Pearson of the Tennessee General Assembly at 7:00 PM. Tickets include food & beverage.

The weekend includes the Blue Palmetto Dinner with Governor Wes Moore, Clyburn’s Fish Fry, and the Saturday convention to elect leadership.

[RSVP here!](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=487b9e8049&e=edbde6c9b1) May 14 Indivisible Upstate Meeting at SCDP. [Register here!](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=317d919010&e=edbde6c9b1)May 20 SPCDP Tiny Town Hall Middle Tyger Library at 6pm. [RSVP here](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=73d2cb7a24&e=edbde6c9b1)!May 29 Fighting for Freedom Fundraiser. [RSVP here!](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=8d68e7a3f5&e=edbde6c9b1)May 30 & 31 South Carolina Democratic Convention. [Learn more & purchase tickets here! ](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=eab5c69e27&e=edbde6c9b1)June 13th-15th Southern Workers Action Summit. [Learn more here!](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=0048efbfd7&e=edbde6c9b1)June 28 Greenville Black Pride Festival. [Learn more here!](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=461ddd2cee&e=edbde6c9b1)
---
|
---
|
---
| | | | | | | | | | |
---
| | **PUBLIC SCHOOL DISTRICTS NEED OUR HELP -**[**CALL YOUR HOUSE REP TODAY**](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=67d68969c3&e=edbde6c9b1) An analysis of school district projections statewide revealed a disproportionate distribution of state funding away from traditional school districts and to the three state charter school organizers. **How can you support your local traditional school districts?** Urge your House Representatives to**amend Proviso 1.3** (SDE: State Aid to Classrooms) to adjust the virtual charter school weighting from.65 to.35 to temporarily mitigate the disproportionate distribution. Urge them to add **a new proviso** to establish an education stakeholders task force made up of representatives from traditional school districts and charter school authorizers that would recommend revisions to ensure a more equitable distribution of funding.
[ **STAND UP AGAINST HOUSE BILL 3309 – PROTECT SOUTH CAROLINA’S ENERGY FUTURE!**](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=551f04b9c5&e=edbde6c9b1) South Carolina lawmakers are once again pushing **House Bill 3309** —and it’s a big step in the wrong direction. This bill gives too much power to monopoly utilities while sticking ratepayers with the bill. It's packed with giveaways to corporations, and it ignores the real energy needs of South Carolinians.[**FLAT TAX BILL UP FOR DEBATE THIS WEEK**](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=5035e78d0b&e=edbde6c9b1)**** [Greenville Republican Rep. Bannister ](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=5cfb94ac55&e=edbde6c9b1)proposed a tax restructuring plan, [**House Bill 4216,**](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=45d5a26f27&e=edbde6c9b1) that would immediately [cut state income taxes](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=c6e1502d89&e=edbde6c9b1). Republicans have long wanted to reduce the state’s top marginal of 6% rate to make South Carolina appear more competitive. The new plan no longer moves immediately to a single, flat tax rate (which the bill did as introduced), rather, **it collapses the state’s three tax rates into two** — eliminating the bottom rate and reducing the top two tiers. Democrats question the need altogether, noting the existing tax structure hasn’t hindered South Carolina from being one of the nation’s fastest-growing states. The bill was up for debate earlier this week, making it **out of committee 17-6,** and will likely be voted on in 2026. Kansas, which recently moved to a flat tax, saw massive cuts to public services and recently set the state on a course to accrue a [**half-billion dollars in debt**](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=00c898e9cb&e=edbde6c9b1) by 2028. North Carolina, which recently enacted a flat tax rate of 4.25 percent, is already [**having challenges**](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=9b8a864b75&e=edbde6c9b1) meeting service obligations, creating new uncertainty at a time President Donald Trump's administration has already begun [**slashing funds**](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=5e69389a76&e=edbde6c9b1) to state health programs and other pricey areas of state budget.
---
|
---
| | | | | | | | | | |
---
| | | | | | | | | | | | The final week of the legislative session is here! **Today, May 8th, marks**[**“sine die,”**](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=792f447dd5&e=edbde6c9b1) a Latin term meaning “without a day,” referring to the adjournment of a session. So, what does this mean for bills? South Carolina operates on a two-year session cycle, so any bills that don’t pass this year can still be considered in the 2026 session.
[**Senate Bill 425**](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=9f2a826592&e=edbde6c9b1) , a pivotal bill that expands access to free, nutritious school meals for students living in poverty, has officially been **ratified and signed by the governor!** This is a huge win in the fight to end hunger, reduce stigma, and ensure that every child in South Carolina has the nourishment they need to succeed in and out of the classroom.
---
| | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://kathrynforcongress.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=25ba2d10590e10adb016803dc&id=1cf77d34c2&e=edbde6c9b1)
|
---
| | | | |
---
| | | | | | | | | | |
---
| | | | | | | | | | |
---
| |
---
| Last night, I had the pleasure of celebrating alongside female candidates from across the United States at the EMILYS List Gala in D.C. The Frogmore Stew crew and I had such an inspiring evening. EMILYs List is a political action committee that aims to support and help elect pro-choice female candidates to office.
---
| | | | | | | | | | |
---
| |
---
| What a meeting on Monday night y’all! We heard from six guest speakers at the monthly Spartanburg Democratic meeting that empowered folks on how to stand up for their immigrant neighbors, what to pay attention to on county council and school boards here locally, the power of due process, the power of workers unions, & what to expect at the upcoming SCDP convention at the end of the month!
---
| | | | | | | | | | |
---

clean_body: The end of the 2025 South Carolina Legislative Session. | | | | | | | | | Welcome to Keeping Up with Kathryn Harvey. A weekly digest covering South Carolina politics and opportunities for engagement and action in the Upstate. | A Hopeful Reflection on Civil Discourse | | | | | | | | | | | The power of convening folks for meaningful dialogue has been on my mind quite a bit recently. As someone who has spent their entire career in strategic communications, I’ve observed both troubling trends and reasons for hope in how we engage with one another on matters of public importance. Politically, it can feel as though we are more divided than ever. However, I have seen glimmers of light here in the Upstate. This past week, Wofford College committed itself as an institution that values diversity of thought, launching series that I have had the pleasure to help host. Our goal is to bring folks together for a moderated discussion aimed at highlighting diverse perspectives while encouraging civil discourse.Thank you to both Bakari Sellers and Trey Gowdy for participating in our first installation. Please save the date for September 29th , where we will be proudly hosting The Sister Senators. | | On this week, we are focusing on this light. Grace Cowan sat down with Upstate’s very own Bill Webster for a rich conversation on how politics has evolved from civil compromise to deep polarization. They explore the legacy of South Carolina political figures, the rise of personalized politics, and what it takes to build respectful dialogue across party lines. Stay tuned for later this week, tomorrow’s episode features a personal conversation with Bakari Sellers. | | | | | | As we come to an arduous end of South Carolina’s legislative session, I am dedicated to holding space for candid conversations amid our polarized political climate. It is more important than ever. Consider joining me locally, on May 20th, for the first | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Fighting for Freedom Fundraiser with Representative Justin PearsonMay 29 at 6:00 pm Chapman Cultural Center Join us for a special evening featuring Rep Justin J. Pearson of the Tennessee Three in support of the Spartanburg Young Democrats. The reception starts at 6:00 PM, followed by a special program honoring our young change makers in the community and a keynote by Rep Justin J. Pearson of the Tennessee General Assembly at 7:00 PM. Tickets include food & beverage. | | | | | | | | | | | | South Carolina Democratic ConventionMay 30-31st in Columbia Join Democrats across South Carolina for the South Carolina Democratic Party's 2025 Convention Weekend. The weekend includes the Blue Palmetto Dinner with Governor Wes Moore, Clyburn’s Fish Fry, and the Saturday convention to elect leadership. | | | | May 12 Democratic Women of Greenville lunch meeting at The Kroc Center. May 14 Indivisible Upstate Meeting at SCDP. 20 SPCDP Tiny Town Hall Middle Tyger Library at 6pm. 29 Fighting for Freedom Fundraiser. 30 & 31 South Carolina Democratic Convention. 13th-15th Southern Workers Action Summit. 28 Greenville Black Pride Festival. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | PUBLIC SCHOOL DISTRICTS NEED OUR HELP - An analysis of school district projections statewide revealed a disproportionate distribution of state funding away from traditional school districts and to the three state charter school organizers. How can you support your local traditional school districts? Urge your House Representatives toamend Proviso 1.3 (SDE: State Aid to Classrooms) to adjust the virtual charter school weighting from.65 to.35 to temporarily mitigate the disproportionate distribution. Urge them to add a new proviso to establish an education stakeholders task force made up of representatives from traditional school districts and charter school authorizers that would recommend revisions to ensure a more equitable distribution of funding. South Carolina lawmakers are once again pushing House Bill 3309 —and it’s a big step in the wrong direction. This bill gives too much power to monopoly utilities while sticking ratepayers with the bill. It's packed with giveaways to corporations, and it ignores the real energy needs of South Carolinians.** a tax restructuring plan, that would immediately . Republicans have long wanted to reduce the state’s top marginal of 6% rate to make South Carolina appear more competitive. The new plan no longer moves immediately to a single, flat tax rate (which the bill did as introduced), rather, it collapses the state’s three tax rates into two — eliminating the bottom rate and reducing the top two tiers. Democrats question the need altogether, noting the existing tax structure hasn’t hindered South Carolina from being one of the nation’s fastest-growing states. The bill was up for debate earlier this week, making it out of committee 17-6, and will likely be voted on in 2026. Kansas, which recently moved to a flat tax, saw massive cuts to public services and recently set the state on a course to accrue a by 2028. North Carolina, which recently enacted a flat tax rate of 4.25 percent, is already meeting service obligations, creating new uncertainty at a time President Donald Trump's administration has already begun to state health programs and other pricey areas of state budget. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | The final week of the legislative session is here! Today, May 8th, marks a Latin term meaning “without a day,” referring to the adjournment of a session. So, what does this mean for bills? South Carolina operates on a two-year session cycle, so any bills that don’t pass this year can still be considered in the 2026 session. , a pivotal bill that expands access to free, nutritious school meals for students living in poverty, has officially been ratified and signed by the governor! This is a huge win in the fight to end hunger, reduce stigma, and ensure that every child in South Carolina has the nourishment they need to succeed in and out of the classroom. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Last night, I had the pleasure of celebrating alongside female candidates from across the United States at the EMILYS List Gala in D.C. The Frogmore Stew crew and I had such an inspiring evening. EMILYs List is a political action committee that aims to support and help elect pro-choice female candidates to office. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | What a meeting on Monday night y’all! We heard from six guest speakers at the monthly Spartanburg Democratic meeting that empowered folks on how to stand up for their immigrant neighbors, what to pay attention to on county council and school boards here locally, the power of due process, the power of workers unions, & what to expect at the upcoming SCDP convention at the end of the month! | | | | | | | | | | | | | | At the Annual Meeting earlier this week, Women Giving for Spartanburg celebrated another year! We awarded $174.999.63** in grants. The first trade deal has been signed with the United Kingdom. President Trump outlined the deal will add $5 Billion new market access on exports and $6 Billion external revenue from tariffs. Scott Bessent will join President Trump in Geneva this weekend where negotiations with China are expected to begin. Joe Biden is doing media rounds trying to salvage his image, the old and frail former president looks older and frailer as he sat next to the ladies of The View, it was mumbley and stumbley babble-fest as he tried to ridicule all that President Trump has accomplished in 100 days. Thankfully Jill was next to him to defend his honor when Biden had trouble answering. Dr. Casey means has been nominated as Surgeon General, replacing Dr. Janette Nesheiwat, who happens to be sister-in-law to Mike Waltz. Department of Transportation is fixing an extremely outdated air traffic control system, Sean Duffy brought the receipts on Chuck Schumer and Buttigeig for allowing infrastructure to deteriorate under the Biden administration. Local D.C. restaurants are freaking out because they don't have the proper paperwork on the illegal immigrant employees they hired. It was another action packed week and we got some winners and losers for you!

name: Shontel Brown
subject: Do you remember Trump’s promise?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Shontel Brown for Congress
body:
I certainly do.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107347402/544394983/1806897946?refcode=EM_F_250508promise_N_SB_90DO_5_NG_N_LOGO&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzg0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmOTA3ZmNjOS01NzJjLWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RV2gkUBPq1V4-MQXD8esSN7tKT3P__QQ8JK_YGskiHY=) Peter, do you remember how Trump promised to lower the cost of goods on Day 1? I certainly do.

We have now endured 100 days since he took office, and he has yet to deliver on that promise. In fact, during that time, he has done NOTHING to help working families and has extracted silence and complicity from GOP lawmakers as they have sat back and **passed the fewest number of bills in decades.**

**Here is what he has been up to instead:** He gutted key government programs – including medical research, safety of food and medicine, and the prevention of gun violence, domestic terror attacks, and violent hate crimes. He’s pardoned the January 6 insurrectionists and attacked the courts, media, and universities. He has insulted our allies and fired thousands of federal workers, repealed anti-discrimination laws, and fired top military leaders in order to replace them with unqualified loyalists.

**He has played games with the livelihoods of millions of people – tanking the economy, raising prices, and pushing us ever closer to a recession. Meanwhile, with Trump’s _Big Disgusting Bill_ he plans to hand over trillions in tax breaks to the ultra-wealthy at the expense of the American people.**

[**We are not going to stand for it. The headlines are growing more alarming by the day. Executive power grabs. Direct threats to the independence of our courts. Donald Trump’s administration is pushing the limits of executive authority — and constitutional experts are warning that a full-blown crisis could be closer than we think. We need to know where you stand: **How concerned are you about a potential constitutional crisis between the executive branch and the courts?**

We need to know where you stand: How concerned are you about a potential constitutional crisis between the executive branch and the courts? = = = = | | | | | | Paid for by The Real Texas PAC, www.therealtexaspac.com, and not authorized by any candidate or candidates committee ABOUT THIS PAC: Contributions or gifts to The Real Texas PAC are not tax deductible. An Individual can contribute up to $5,000 to The Real Texas PAC per calendar year. Married couples may together give a total of $10,000 per calendar year. Federal political action committees (PACs) can contribute up to $5,000 per calendar year. The first $5,000 contributed in a calendar year from federally permissible sources will be deposited in The Real Texas PAC's contribution account. Once a federally permissible source has contributed $5,000 in a calendar year, The Real Texas PAC will transfer any amounts in excess of $5,000 to its non-contribution account. Peter, just yesterday, Trump attacked me from the Oval Office on live TV – this time calling me “a total crook,” “a horrible, horrible human being,” and “a sick person.” He even suggested the Department of Justice will come after me.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107348377/544398391/-2022258813?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk1943-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTg0OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0Y2NlYmI5MS01YTJjLWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2xBlf0Og_jWg4QyJRDX3DFX5RpzTojOVEbI2SB-he9E=)
Let me be clear: This is not just another outburst.

It’s part of a coordinated smear campaign – from Trump to Stephen Miller to Alex Jones to the full weight of the MAGA machine – all working to discredit me for doing my job and holding them accountable.

# **Still Feeling the Biden Hangover?**
---
| | |
---
The last administration got drunk on spending, and it’s STILL poisoning the economy. **You’re not imagining it —** Biden’s binge wiped out 20% of your buying power. And for years, we've been mixing bad decisions with stimulus spending and easy money... **So it’s no wonder the economy’s hung over.** We know Trump’s doing his best to fix the mess left behind by Biden... But now, Mike Johnson says Trump’s strategy to restore the economy is “going to work.” **Then in my recent interview with President Trump he said:**
---
[](https://email.email.america1stwarroom.com/c/eJxkkE-P0zAQxT-Nc3PlzCS2e8ihu9mChLRsixCCC3JtpzHYSRrnj_rtkdulQmKOb37z3syoMHjXXCsinghA60wkAAR3BODyfQ3j_Ct8-kDN5-IHvfDXUKYuPL-jCzzgTqvj8vbFvr59VHF3MM8XXhyW_IFP18E-4PfQjQ3K-aSKOjMVNoYVma1yUXBRYI6QtdVWNFxaA4VSJ8mg4cANNIgc8cShZJmrgEHJSiYBAJFvrJCccatsbuRWA5CC3XI2KtjRaZXHaVXj2Pdho_uQ-aqdpiHeFtsT2K_rujn33kTnFzv-dhO9T-s-ENi3quvcdKWJoHeEJubkZ0sZ5hLKZIP72GhnCNaC5YeWpfp6eTruCHAfCdaM5fQl-dJvaqTHPplz3RGsZVlSvhVUMlkS4PMUfsZ-HrUlWP_DJj1Y4-ZAsH65__EmahUG5c7d_9nZWJlhdd67yAUp2PnvXdlSwZ8AAAD__8jKnNY)
---
| | And this gives us hope, because he continues, "**_It's a restructuring of the American economy. What we're doing is building a foundation for the future._** " But if this restructuring of America takes six months... Then Trump gave you the heads-up you need to ride it out. So If there’s a practical way to prepare, without betting against America, then it’s worth knowing about. Optimism shouldn’t mean ignoring reality. You’re not imagining it — Biden’s binge wiped out 20% of your buying power. And for years, we've been mixing bad decisions with stimulus spending and easy money... So it’s no wonder the economy’s hung over. We know Trump’s doing his best to fix the mess left behind by Biden... name: Shawnna Bolick
subject: May 8th Legislative Update
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VOTE BOLICK
body:
| | | [ ](https://bolickforarizona.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3214fdbd528e32d06cf84fc86&id=68d15fc3f8&e=a7ab36849c)
---
| | Good afternoon!

We are well over the 100-day mark at the Capitol. These past two weeks the legislature has refocused its efforts on legislation to extend Prop 123 and look at what type of state budget we might be able to pass and get signed before we Sine Die by the end of June.

In more positive news, I have had the opportunity to meet with some of our Senate interns and pages who will be wrapping up their time with us soon. They have been an amazing asset to our committees and while we are conducting work on the Senate floor.

Last week, I finally made it over to a [second chance reentry graduation](https://bolickforarizona.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3214fdbd528e32d06cf84fc86&id=01425aa7e6&e=a7ab36849c), in the Phoenix area. This program equips incarcerated men with the skills needed to start over when their sentence has been completed.

During my tenure, I have sponsored bills to pre-empt our state’s cities and towns from government overreach, including getting rid of the [municipal tax on food](https://bolickforarizona.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3214fdbd528e32d06cf84fc86&id=826044e77a&e=a7ab36849c) for human consumption in 2019. Then, in 2022, prohibiting cities from levying a [residential rental tax](https://bolickforarizona.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3214fdbd528e32d06cf84fc86&id=d76cb2c660&e=a7ab36849c). I have never shied away from going after this particular group of lobbyists who represent cities, not people.

This year we have been actively working on a deregulation housing bill aimed to reduce restrictions imposed by cities, providing greater flexibility for home buyers in areas with populations over 70,000 with the goal of making housing more affordable. As we have learned over the years, municipalities are imposing specific design standards on certain single-family homes and even mandating home builders create HOAs to get approval to build homes across the state.

Sadly, these same leftwing lobbyists who show up at the Capitol each legislative session to lobby against mostly good Republican legislation not only seem to be “[running the governor’s office](https://bolickforarizona.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3214fdbd528e32d06cf84fc86&id=28eb655ac5&e=a7ab36849c), or at least her staff,” but even worse they have managed to co-opt local Republican committees into thinking price controls in the housing sector are the solution in today's free market economy. Funny enough, the first iteration of a published article in one of the local political news prints correctly stated what I said above in quotes, but was removed from a [subsequent edition](https://bolickforarizona.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3214fdbd528e32d06cf84fc86&id=18eb0e209b&e=a7ab36849c). I wonder if the staff on the 9th floor called the reporter to request my quote be removed.

Thankfully, I represent people, not cities. I will continue to work with my constituents on solving these issues, not a group of taxpayer-funded lobbyists who don’t care about the next generation of homeownership. Furthermore, this same leftwing group of lobbyists are no different than the education unions. I really hope our local Republican committees are smart enough to block a potential insurgence of the NEA into their meetings.

Speaking of unions, we can't let these out of state unions from California overrun Arizona. They have successfully driven out the Arizona Coyotes NHL team and are slowly chipping away at our state's right to work status by teaming up with so called Republicans in Scottsdale to reject our state's future prosperity. Here's a great recently published [story](https://bolickforarizona.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3214fdbd528e32d06cf84fc86&id=5eb720cfcf&e=a7ab36849c) that is on point.

**Bolick Bills Recently Signed by the Governor**

[SB 1103](https://bolickforarizona.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3214fdbd528e32d06cf84fc86&id=f39415bad6&e=a7ab36849c)
penalty assessment; victims' rights enforcement

[SB 1107](https://bolickforarizona.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3214fdbd528e32d06cf84fc86&id=89e94b29e9&e=a7ab36849c)
Motorcycle safety fund continuation

[SB 1343](https://bolickforarizona.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3214fdbd528e32d06cf84fc86&id=db8f373400&e=a7ab36849c)
Probation supervision; transfer

[SB 1449](https://bolickforarizona.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3214fdbd528e32d06cf84fc86&id=7ebad31afe&e=a7ab36849c)
lifetime injunction; undesignated offenses

SB 1527
occupational boards; renewal extension

[SB 1659](https://bolickforarizona.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3214fdbd528e32d06cf84fc86&id=5260f295e1&e=a7ab36849c)
State board; allegations of misconduct

_Bolick Legislation in the News_

[https://cronkitenews.azpbs.org/2025/04/29/arizona-legislators-law-enforcement-animal-cruelty-laws-bill/](https://bolickforarizona.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3214fdbd528e32d06cf84fc86&id=c387cf9b94&e=a7ab36849c)

[https://www.abc15.com/news/local-news/arizona-legislators-and-law-enforcement-want-better-defined-animal-cruelty-laws](https://bolickforarizona.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3214fdbd528e32d06cf84fc86&id=a5ab62905d&e=a7ab36849c)

_Campaign Information_

Do you reside in one of the following zip codes?
85022

85027

85331

If you answered yes and you are a registered Republican or an Independent voter, you live in Legislative District 2, so [**please ****take a minute to ****sign my online nomination petition**](https://bolickforarizona.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3214fdbd528e32d06cf84fc86&id=716cf031c2&e=a7ab36849c) ensuring my name appears on the Republican primary ballot. After session, we will actively work to accomplish this goal. Today, you can use your driver's license to log on and sign my online nomination petition.

Thank you for your continued support!

Take care,
Shawnna

name: Chrissy Houlahan
subject: What my team and I have been up to
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chrissy Houlahan for Congress
body:
My updates from the last few months.
| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107348671/544399522/-1072566757?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NDkxMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZjU4ZTFmYi01YjJjLWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=GH1q6XUErDI90btRLWVckfeA0SztFmQ_HE5VW5tdWYk=&emci=8616e021-502c-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&emdi=5f58e1fb-5b2c-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=21278485)
---
| | **A NOTE FROM CHRISSY**
---
| | **my bipartisan bill PASSED THE HOUSE**
---
There isn’t a lot that Democrats and Republicans in Congress agree on these days but one glimmer of hope was the unanimous passage of my bill, the Mathematical and Statistical Modeling Education Act. This bill will provide grants to K-12 schools to support innovative STEM curricula, ensuring our students can become the best in the world and are not left behind in the global economy. Now, I urge the Senate to also pass this bill so we can get it signed into law. Learn more about the bill [**here**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107348672/544399525/-317993812?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NDkxMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZjU4ZTFmYi01YjJjLWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=GH1q6XUErDI90btRLWVckfeA0SztFmQ_HE5VW5tdWYk=&emci=8616e021-502c-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&emdi=5f58e1fb-5b2c-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=21278485).
---
| | **HUMANITARIAN AID TO GAZA**
---
As famine spreads and the loss of life continues, I joined almost 100 of my fellow Democrats in Congress to call on the Israeli government to lift the blockade of humanitarian assistance into Gaza. While I share concerns about Hamas diverting the aid, I believe the United States, the Israeli government, and humanitarian organizations on the ground should do what they can to minimize the risk and get the relief to Palestinian civilians.
---
| | **PAID LEAVE IN PENNSYLVANIA**
---
I’ve been a leader on pushing paid leave policies at the federal level. In fact, I introduced bipartisan legislation last week on paid leave - legislation that was more than two years in the making. The Pennsylvania House Labor and Industry Committee has advanced the Family Care Act, a bill to create a paid family leave program in our state and I'm encouraged to see paid leave progressing in Pennsylvania. I urge State Rep. McClinton to bring this bill to the statehouse floor for a vote to show the rest of the nation what’s possible and provide this crucial benefit to Pennsylvanians. I’ll keep doing the same in Washington.
---
| | **I VOTED "NO" ON THE SAVE ACT**
---
In April, House GOP Representatives passed the SAVE Act, an extremely misleading bill that will make it more difficult for all Americans to vote – _especially women and troops stationed overseas_. This is not voter reform, it's voter suppression – and it _will_ impact thousands of people in Berks and Chester counties. As your elected representative, working tirelessly to protect our democracy, this was an easy NO vote for me.
---
| | **SIGNALGATE**
---
We learned that senior national security officials, including Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, shared war planning information over a Signal group chat, putting our national security and the lives of troops at risk. Like you, I was shocked.

As an Air Force veteran and a member of the House Intelligence Committee, I understand – to my core – how reckless and dangerous this behavior is. That's why I introduced legislation to require Secretary Hegseth to do the right thing and step down. I’m fighting to hold those responsible accountable – and I won't stop until they are.
---
| | **PENN STATE BERKS COMMENCEMENT**
---
On Saturday, I have the honor of speaking to this year’s Penn State Berks graduating class. It's an absolute privilege to speak to the next generation of leaders, and I can't thank Penn State enough for having me.
---
I wish you a terrific Mother’s Day weekend. There's work to be done,

Chrissy
